positives: Programmable start time lets you schedule a brew in advance, so coffee is ready when you wake up
Count-up timer displays how long the coffee has been sitting in the carafe
Five-port showerhead disperses water evenly over the coffee bed for consistent extraction
Automatic pre-infusion cycle adjusts bloom time, water quantity, and overall brewing time based on batch size
Silicone mixing tube directs freshly brewed coffee to the bottom of the pot to keep it evenly mixed
Thermal stainless steel carafe brews directly through its lid and kept coffee at about 180 °F after one hour
Measured extraction at 22.5 percent on our best-brewed pot
Plastic parts (filter basket, showerhead, carafe head, mixing tube) are dishwasher safe and the machine reminds you to descale every 90 brews
negatives: Brew bed temperature measured about 195 °F during brewing, slightly below the SCA-approved range of 197.6–204.8 °F
Nonintuitive push-and-dial interface requires scrolling through label-free options and consulting the manual
Carafe lid traps old coffee residue and is difficult to clean without a pipe cleaner
Water-delivery mechanism retains water after brewing and drips into the filter basket, creating a perpetually wet environment
Overall machine height of just over 17 inches may not fit under standard kitchen cabinets

positives: Maintained stable brew bed temperature of 200.4 °F after two minutes of brewing
Flat-bottom filter basket contributed to more even extraction than cone-shaped filters
Pre-infusion mode primes the grounds for brewing (activated by holding the power button for five seconds)
Brews a six-cup pot in 4 minutes, 30 seconds
On-off rocker switch flips back to off when brewing completes for simple, foolproof operation
Compact height of 12 inches fits under most upper cabinets
Achieved a 19.44 percent extraction on the sweetest, most balanced pot we brewed
negatives: Lacks programmability—no timer function for scheduled brews
Requires screwing the carafe lid on after brewing before pouring, adding an extra, inconvenient step
Lidded carafe must be removed and reattached to fit back under the brew basket or to refill the reservoir
Carafe cooled from 185 °F to 172 °F over the course of an hour, showing lower heat retention than the top pick

positives: Brew-through carafe lid allows the carafe to stay closed and insulated while brewing
Compact footprint (about 11 inches long by 6 inches wide) conserves counter space
Simple operation—add ground coffee and water, then flip the switch to brew
Has a dedicated following, and some Wirecutter staffers have used it as their primary brewer for years
negatives: Lacks programmability features such as a timer or descaling reminders
Omits convenience features like a digital interface and automatic pre-infusion controls
High price is not justified for the limited feature set for most users

positives: Met recommended guidelines of 195°F or more, maintained for five or six minutes.
Excelled in CR's brew performance test, maintaining an optimal brewing temperature for the best coffee concentration.
Earns a very good score for brew performance, meaning it hits the temperature required for optimal flavor.
Delivers a pot in about 11 minutes.
BREW PERFORMANCE: Met recommended guidelines of 195°F or more, maintained for five or six minutes.
Met recommended guidelines of 195°F or more, maintained for five or six minutes in brew performance.
One of the least expensive drip coffee makers in CR’s tests, and it’s CR Recommended, indicating it meets our highest standards.

records.raw_product_name: Bunn HB Heat N Brew Programmable
positives: Receives strong marks across the board in our performance tests.

records.raw_product_name: Technivorm Moccamaster Cup-One Brewer Coffee Maker

positives: Allows users to adjust brew temperature, time, and flow rate for customizable brewing options.
Brews coffee at an optimal range of 195°F to 205°F, ensuring full flavor extraction from coffee grounds.
Features a slow drip option for precision brewing favored by coffee aficionados.
Specializes in single-serve brewing, allowing users to brew a fresh cup without needing a full pot.
Heats water to the ideal temperature for optimal extraction.
Design ensures consistent brewing results across cups.
Features a programmable timer and a 14-cup capacity for flexible scheduling and large-batch brewing.
Brew Pause feature allows users to pour a cup before the full brewing cycle completes.
Test results indicate it brews coffee with good flavor consistency across multiple trials.

records.raw_product_name: Braun KF7150BK BrewSense Drip Coffee Maker
positives: Features an easy-to-read display and a programmable option for simple operation.
Unique brewing system delivers consistent results and preserves flavor.
Sleek design and reliability make it suitable for various kitchen styles.

records.raw_product_name: Mr. Coffee Optimal Brew Thermal Coffee Maker
positives: Keeps brewed coffee hot for hours with thermal insulation for extended heat retention.
Stainless-steel thermal carafe prevents burning and promotes flavor integrity without a hot plate.
Suitable for users who prefer hot coffee without risking over-brewing from a hot plate.
Employs a blooming process that pours a small amount of hot water over the grounds before brewing, encouraging the release of carbon dioxide and aromatic oils for a more flavorful extraction.

Brews a full 40-ounce carafe (about 10 cups) in just over seven minutes, operating at 196 to 205°F and producing coffee at around 180°F, maintaining it at about 177°F for approximately one hour and 40 minutes before shutting off.
Includes a five-year warranty and lifetime repair guarantee, reducing long-term replacement costs.
negatives: Its narrow showerhead may limit extraction of richer and bolder flavors compared to models with wider showerheads.
Offers limited customization beyond half- or full-pot brewing options, which may disappoint users seeking more control.
Features loose parts such as a detachable water tank and basket lids that add extra steps to cleaning and preparation.

positives: Consistently produces good, flavorful coffee while remaining under a $100 price point.
Features a warming plate that stays on for up to four hours, maintaining coffee temperatures between 188 and 192°F.
Offers a programmable 24-hour delay brew and a '1-4 cups' button, catering to single households.
Delivers a full 12-cup carafe at a piping hot 195°F.
negatives: The warming plate’s maintained temperature of 188 to 192°F can negatively impact coffee taste over time.
The brew cycle takes almost nine minutes for a full 12-cup carafe, which may be too slow for users seeking faster brewing.

positives: Typical extractions reach 167°F, and using its double-walled thermal carafe, coffee cools to 145°F after 1.5 hours, aligning with ideal drinking temperatures.
Completes brews in just over seven minutes and offers expert-curated presets, downloadable brew profiles, and a single-serve mode for customizable brewing.
Includes 15 cone and 15 flat-bottom filters and comes with a two-year standard warranty (three years when registered).
Has a removable water tank and a filter basket that stops dripping when removed, making cleaning effortless.
negatives: Lacks a warming plate, resulting in cooler coffee (145°F) after 1.5 hours, which may disappoint users who prefer piping hot drinks.
Occupies significant counter space, which may be problematic in smaller kitchens.

positives: Brews a full 40-ounce carafe in under 5 minutes and 30 seconds at nearly 182°F, faster than the Technivorm Moccamaster.
Maintains coffee temperature at 140°F after one hour in its glass carafe, despite lacking a warming plate.
Offers effortless one-touch operation with a single massive touch button that toggles between half and full batch modes.
Design allows the carafe and basket to fit as a single unit for easy removal and cleaning.
negatives: Does not include a warming plate, which may limit heat retention for users who desire hotter coffee.
Carries a premium price tag of $799 to $899, which may be prohibitive for budget-conscious buyers.
Requires removing the basket from the carafe when pouring, necessitating the use of an included catcher for drip control.

positives: It is Specialty Coffee Association-approved.
It automatically stops drips to prevent mess during brewing.
It comes with a five-year warranty and lifetime repair support.
It offers both half-capacity and full-capacity brewing options.
negatives: It lacks delayed brew modes for setting a future start time.

positives: It is extremely easy to use, requiring minimal button presses to start a brew.
It supports both single-cup and carafe brewing for flexible serving sizes.
It brews consistently good coffee, backed by its Specialty Coffee Association certification.
It features a thermal carafe that keeps coffee hot for hours.
Its compact size makes it suitable for small kitchens.
negatives: The water tank isn’t removable, making refills less convenient.
It is not programmable, so you cannot schedule a brew in advance.

positives: It has a premium, space-saving design that fits in like an electric kettle.
Its companion app is excellent, allowing real-time monitoring and detailed customization.
It offers unparalleled custom brewing functions with three modes: Autopilot, Copilot, and Freesolo.
It includes a very capable grinder with 80 grind settings and a 48 mm conical burr operating at 60–120 RPM.
It features a sensitive scale for precise dosing of up to 25 grams of coffee.
negatives: Its maximum dose setting makes batch brewing tricky.
It can be a little fiddly to use at first due to unlabelled dials.

records.raw_product_name: Gevi 10-Cup Drip Coffee Maker with Grinder
positives: It is affordably priced compared to standalone coffee maker and grinder combinations.
Its touchscreen interface is easy to use and modern.
negatives: Its entirely plastic construction raises durability and safety concerns.
It advises against using dark roast beans, as their oils will destroy the grinder.
A 10-cup brew takes 16 minutes, which is slow for a drip brewer.
Coffee tastes either bitter or watery with no consistency due to grinder issues.
